name: Parabolic Reflector
description: A parabolic reflector antenna consists of a parabolic-shaped conducting surface that focuses electromagnetic waves to or from a feed antenna located at the focal point. The parabolic geometry provides high directivity and gain by creating a uniform phase front across the aperture when illuminated by a point source at the focus.

diameter.name: Reflector Diameter
diameter.description: Physical diameter of the parabolic reflector aperture

focal_length.name: Focal Length
focal_length.description: Distance from reflector vertex to focal point where feed is located

f_over_d.name: F/D Ratio
f_over_d.description: Ratio of focal length to diameter, determines reflector depth and feed pattern requirements

edge_taper.name: Edge Taper
edge_taper.description: Reduction in illumination amplitude at reflector edge compared to center

surface_tolerance.name: Surface Tolerance
surface_tolerance.description: RMS deviation of reflector surface from ideal parabola

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting reflector geometry, determining feed requirements, optimizing illumination taper, and analyzing performance trade-offs between gain, sidelobe level, and bandwidth.

steps.title: Determine Aperture Size
steps.description: Calculate required diameter based on gain specification

steps.title: Select F/D Ratio
steps.description: Choose focal length to diameter ratio based on feed pattern and blockage constraints

steps.title: Calculate Focal Length
steps.description: Determine focal point location from reflector vertex

steps.title: Design Feed Illumination
steps.description: Select feed pattern to achieve desired edge taper

steps.title: Optimize Edge Taper
steps.description: Balance gain and sidelobe performance through illumination control
steps.formula: G_max occurs at TE = -8.7 dB for uniform phase

steps.title: Analyze Blockage Effects
steps.description: Account for feed and support structure shadowing

equations.gain: G = eta_ap * (pi*D/lambda)^2 where eta_ap is aperture efficiency
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta,phi) = (j*k*exp(-j*k*r)/(4*pi*r)) * integral over aperture of E_aperture(x',y') * exp(j*k*(x'*sin(theta)*cos(phi) + y'*sin(theta)*sin(phi)))
equations.bandwidth: BW limited by feed bandwidth and focal point shift with frequency

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use feed antenna impedance model with reflector coupling effects
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Physical optics aperture integration with Fresnel diffraction corrections
keyAssumptions: Negligible edge diffraction for large D/lambda

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', Chapter 15
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', Chapter 12
